milestones.notes: Native OpenClaw plugin direction, OWS security model, and cron-based periodic agent spec are documented.
Repo-level guidance, script entrypoints, and initial eval matrix exist.
Package manifest, plugin manifest, entrypoint, CLI registrar, workspace generation, profile persistence, and vendored skills are now implemented.

scripts/dev/sandbox creates an isolated data root, materializes a fixture profile, runs runRebalance against canned Morpho data (planned/no-op/blocked scenarios), and cleans up on exit. scripts/dev/doctor exercises the planned scenario as a probe.

OWS policy creation, OpenClaw agent/cron orchestration, Morpho reads, deterministic dry-run planning, and the OWS-sign-plus-broadcast live wrapper are implemented. Morpho adapter rewritten to match real v2026.4 @morpho-org/cli shapes; live read path verified end-to-end via scripts/check/live-path.

Wallet setup, risk profile selection, policy generation, funding guidance with balance polling, optional model-preference routing, workspace generation, cron setup, and a final in-wizard validation dry-run are all implemented.

Plugin register phase uses OpenClaw's normalizeResolvedSecretInputString to detect pre-resolved SecretRefs (via apiKeyValue) and register an inline token source. Env and file SecretRef shapes also map into our native env/file resolvers. SEC-001 eval exercises the inline + env + file pathways.

Scoring now applies per-profile weights for APY, normalized TVL, fee percentage, and a rewards penalty for reward-averse profiles. The breakdown is persisted on each selected vault in the run receipt.

package.json now declares a files allowlist that excludes state/progress.json, evals/, docs/, src/bin/evals|doctor, and dev scripts. scripts/check/publish runs npm pack --dry-run and enforces required + forbidden entries. scripts/dev/doctor surfaces the same check.

CFG-001/002, WAL-001, CRN-001/002, REB-001..REB-004, OBS-001, POL-002, and POL-003 are all executable via scripts/check/evals. POL-001 remains an operator-visible confirmation (no extractable in-process assertion).
runRebalance emits JSONL phase events (start/read/plan/prepare/execute/verify/complete/error) via src/lib/run-logger.ts with secret redaction. The result now carries logPath alongside receiptPath.

checks.notes: Security and spec invariants are present in repository docs.
scripts/dev/up now delegates to scripts/dev/doctor, which probes tool availability, typecheck, evals, and token source. Current environment reports openclaw and ows on PATH.
Produces stable text/JSON health summary. Exits non-zero on fail; warnings like unset default token env var are reported without failing the run.
Dry-run executed against a temporary profile and live Morpho data, returned a planned rebalance, and wrote a receipt JSON.
Script refuses live execution unless VAULT_MANAGER_ALLOW_LIVE=1 is set.
Fixture-driven scenarios REB-001 through REB-004 execute against runRebalance with injected Morpho read deps and pass in CI.
The native OpenClaw plugin package and CLI implementation typecheck successfully.
The Morpho CLI invocation is fixed, but this environment still lacks openclaw and ows on PATH.
Blocked by missing openclaw on PATH in the current environment.
The configure wizard now shows the wallet address, polls the USDC balance via Morpho reads, and persists the last funded-check probe onto the profile.
Model preference flows end-to-end: captured in configure, stored on the profile, written into AGENTS.md, and passed to openclaw agents add --model plus openclaw cron add --model.
After saving the profile, the wizard runs a dry-run rebalance and persists the validation run summary back onto the profile.
Token sources (env + file/singleValue + file/json) resolve through src/lib/secrets.ts. Configure probes the source live before finishing, and showStatus surfaces readiness from the actual resolver.
npm pack --dry-run --json output is parsed by scripts/check/publish to enforce allowlist/denylist invariants. Integrated into scripts/dev/doctor.

End-to-end read path verified: preflight, openclaw/ows/morpho-cli versions, gateway status, query-vaults + get-vault + get-positions + get-token-balance against real Base state, dry-run rebalance against a live profile, and live-runner refusal without arming. Exposed as a doctor probe.

details: openclaw and ows are both on PATH in the current development environment
src/lib/rebalance.ts only iterates vaultPositions; marketPositions from Morpho reads are silently dropped.
Spec §Agent Behavior Contract narrows the product to USDC vault positions only.
Add an explicit assertion or structured warning if marketPositions is non-empty so the narrow-by-design invariant is enforced, not implicit.
src/cli/configure.ts prints an `ows api-key create` command for the operator to run instead of executing it, to avoid pulling the token into plugin process memory.
Spec §Configure Flow Step 2 reads as if provisioning is automated.

Reconcile by either (a) updating docs/openclaw-vault-manager-spec.md to document the manual provisioning trade-off, or (b) running the command through runCommand and immediately dropping the token into the configured SecretRef path.
src/lib/rebalance.ts records simulationOk=false and continues preparing subsequent actions in the same run.
Spec §Execution Path says the run should stop and report failure with no fallback heuristics.
Short-circuit the action loop on the first prepare/simulation failure and emit a single structured error in the run receipt + JSONL log.
pause/resume/run-now are fully implemented in src/cli/register.ts but spec §Rollout Phases still labels them Phase 2.
Update docs/openclaw-vault-manager-spec.md §Rollout Phases to move these into Phase 1 so the shipping spec matches what the plugin actually delivers.
src/cli/configure.ts stores modelPreference=undefined when the operator picks 'inherit', so the profile is not a complete audit trail of what model actually ran.
Resolve the active OpenClaw default at configure time (via `openclaw config get` or equivalent) and snapshot it onto the profile alongside an `inheritedAt` timestamp.
